title: Coffee and brown sugar body scrub
subtitle: A coarse exfoliating scrub with spent coffee grounds
excerpt: A body scrub made from spent coffee grounds, brown sugar, and coconut oil. The coffee grounds provide firm exfoliation and contain caffeine, which may temporarily improve skin texture. Spent grounds are used rather than fresh coffee; the batch uses what would otherwise be bin-bound.
sourceNotes: Bramble Berry body scrub formulation notes (brambleberry.com, May 2026). Essential Oil Safety, Tisserand and Young (2014), vanilla absolute notes.

content.text: A coffee and brown sugar body scrub. Spent coffee grounds from a morning brew, mixed with brown sugar and melted coconut oil. The coffee grounds provide the coarse physical exfoliation; the brown sugar provides a finer secondary exfoliation and a light caramel scent; the coconut oil is the carrier that carries both exfoliants and rinses clean from the skin and the shower.

Spent coffee grounds contain residual moisture, which makes this scrub perishable. Keep it refrigerated and use within 2 weeks. For a longer-shelf-life version, use fresh, dry ground coffee instead of spent grounds; the scrub keeps 3 months in a cool, dark cupboard without the moisture from used grounds.

items.name: Ground coffee
items.prepNote: spent coffee grounds, spread on a tray and air-dried for 1-2 hours after brewing. Still slightly damp is acceptable; completely wet is not.

items.name: Light brown sugar
items.prepNote: soft dark brown sugar. Finer exfoliant than the coffee grounds; dissolves gradually during use.

items.name: Coconut oil
items.prepNote: refined coconut oil (solid at room temperature, melts on skin contact). Melt gently before adding to the dry ingredients.

items.name: Vanilla extract
items.prepNote: optional. Pure vanilla extract at 1-2% of the batch for a warm, bakery-like scent. Reduces the shelf life slightly.

content.text: If the coconut oil is solid, warm it briefly in a bowl over hot water or in a microwave in 15-second pulses until just melted. Allow to cool slightly; it should be liquid but not hot when it goes into the scrub.

Weigh the coffee grounds and brown sugar into a bowl and stir to combine. Pour the melted coconut oil over the dry ingredients and mix thoroughly with a fork or spatula until all the dry ingredients are coated. The mixture should hold together loosely when pressed; if it's too crumbly, add more coconut oil in 10 g increments. If using vanilla extract, stir it through at this point. Transfer to a clean, dry jar.

Scoop a generous tablespoon of scrub onto damp skin in the shower. Massage in circular motions over the body, avoiding the face (the coffee grounds are too coarse for facial skin). Rinse off with warm water. The coconut oil leaves a light residue on the skin; pat dry. Use a small amount of residual towel oil on dry patches.

Coffee grounds block shower drains over time. Lay an old sieve or a drain guard over the drain before using the scrub, or scoop the spent grounds from the shower floor into the bin rather than rinsing them down.

2 weeks maximum in the fridge. The spent coffee grounds contain residual moisture that can cause mould in an unrefrigerated jar. Always use dry hands or a clean spoon to scoop from the jar; never allow shower water into the container. Discard if any surface mould appears or if the coconut oil smells rancid.
